factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in the Freight Segment driven by higher international locomotive sales and increased demand for services and components.

evidence.text: Freight Segment organic sales increased by $244 million driven primarily by: • Equipment sales from higher international locomotive sales • Components sales from higher original equipment railcar build, more railcars in operation and growth in industrial end-markets • Services sales from a larger active locomotive fleet, partially offset by lower deliveries of locomotive modernizations • Digital Intelligence sales due to higher demand for on-board locomotive and mining solutions and technology upgrades and international PTC sales.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating, particularly in the Freight Segment, driven by higher international locomotive sales and increased demand for services and components.

factors.summary: The company is implementing price escalations and surcharges to mitigate cost increases, indicating pricing power is being maintained.

evidence.text: The Company utilizes various mitigating actions intended to lessen the impact of macroeconomic volatility. These actions include implementing price escalations and surcharges, driving operational efficiencies through various cost mitigation efforts and discretionary spend management, strategically sourcing materials, reviewing and modifying distribution logistics, and accelerating integration synergies through Integration 2.0.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is maintaining pricing power through price escalations and surcharges to mitigate cost increases.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Freight Segment, which is growing faster than the Transit Segment.

evidence.text: Freight Segment organic sales increased by $244 million driven primarily by: • Equipment sales from higher international locomotive sales • Components sales from higher original equipment railcar build, more railcars in operation and growth in industrial end-markets • Services sales from a larger active locomotive fleet, partially offset by lower deliveries of locomotive modernizations • Digital Intelligence sales due to higher demand for on-board locomotive and mining solutions and technology upgrades and international PTC sales.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Freight Segment, which is growing faster than the Transit Segment.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the Freight Segment, which saw a 18.5% increase in net sales.

evidence.text: Net sales for the three months ended March 31, 2023 increased by $267 million, or 13.9%, to $2.19 billion compared to the same period in 2022. Organic sales increased $302 million which was primarily attributable to the Freight Segment driven by an increase across all product lines.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the Freight Segment, with a 18.5% increase in net sales.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the Freight Segment, driven by higher international locomotive sales and increased service activity.

evidence.text: Freight Segment organic sales increased by $244 million driven primarily by: • Equipment sales from higher international locomotive sales • Components sales from higher original equipment railcar build, more railcars in operation and growth in industrial end-markets • Services sales from a larger active locomotive fleet, partially offset by lower deliveries of locomotive modernizations • Digital Intelligence sales due to higher demand for on-board locomotive and mining solutions and technology upgrades and international PTC sales.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the Freight Segment, driven by higher international locomotive sales and increased service activity.

factors.summary: The Freight Segment is growing fastest, driven by higher international locomotive sales and increased demand for services and components, while the Transit Segment is growing more slowly.

evidence.text: Freight Segment organic sales increased by $244 million driven primarily by: • Equipment sales from higher international locomotive sales • Components sales from higher original equipment railcar build, more railcars in operation and growth in industrial end-markets • Services sales from a larger active locomotive fleet, partially offset by lower deliveries of locomotive modernizations • Digital Intelligence sales due to higher demand for on-board locomotive and mining solutions and technology upgrades and international PTC sales.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The Freight Segment is growing fastest, driven by higher international locomotive sales and increased demand for services and components.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to supply chain disruptions, inflation, and geopolitical factors, but the company is mitigating these through operational efficiencies and restructuring.

evidence.text: Supply chain disruptions and labor availability have caused component, raw material and chip shortages resulting in an adverse effect on the timing of the Company’s revenue and cash flows. Additionally, broad-based inflation, metals, energy and other commodity costs, transportation and logistics costs, labor costs, and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations all continue to impact our results.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising due to supply chain disruptions, inflation, and geopolitical factors, creating margin pressure despite mitigating actions.

factors.summary: Labor costs are creating margin pressure due to wage inflation and union negotiations.

evidence.text: A portion of our workers are represented by labor unions. For 2023, the United Electrical, Radio and Machine Workers of America (UE), Locals 506 and 618 collective bargaining agreement, covering approximately 1,400 locomotive manufacturing workers in Erie, Pennsylvania, expires on June 9, 2023. Negotiations with UE will officially begin on April 27, 2023. The Company continuously monitors its labor activity.
evidence.subsection: Item 2.    MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are creating margin pressure due to wage inflation and ongoing union negotiations.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are stable with some compression due to cost increases and restructuring charges.

evidence.text: Cost of sales as a percentage of net sales was 69.7% and 69.1% for the three months ended March 31, 2023 and 2022, respectively. The increase was due to unfavorable product mix between business groups and higher next generation product development costs in Digital Intelligence. These increases were partially offset by operational efficiencies resulting in favorable productivity and higher absorption rates, benefits from structured cost actions taken through Integration 2.0 and prior years restructuring and integration projects, and the effects of foreign exchange rates.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are stable but facing compression due to cost increases and restructuring charges.

factors.summary: Restructuring programs are delivering measurable savings, with $9 million incurred in the first quarter of 2023.

evidence.text: The Company anticipates that it will incur one-time restructuring charges of approximately $135 million to $165 million related to this initiative, of which approximately $78 million has been incurred through March 31, 2023. Total estimated initiative charges could change based on the specific programs approved or changes to the scope of the review. During three months ended March 31, 2023, the Company incurred one-time restructuring charges for programs included in the initiative of approximately $9 million, primarily for employee-related costs and asset write downs associated with site consolidations in Europe.
evidence.subsection: Item 2.    MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring programs are delivering measurable savings, indicating cost efficiency.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving due to restructuring actions and productivity gains.

evidence.text: Cost of sales as a percentage of net sales was 69.7% and 69.1% for the three months ended March 31, 2023 and 2022, respectively. The increase was due to unfavorable product mix between business groups and higher next generation product development costs in Digital Intelligence. These increases were partially offset by operational efficiencies resulting in favorable productivity and higher absorption rates, benefits from structured cost actions taken through Integration 2.0 and prior years restructuring and integration projects, and the effects of foreign exchange rates.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency is improving, driven by restructuring and productivity gains.

factors.summary: The supply chain is still disrupted, with component, raw material, and chip shortages impacting revenue and cash flows.

evidence.text: Supply chain disruptions and labor availability have caused component, raw material and chip shortages resulting in an adverse effect on the timing of the Company’s revenue and cash flows. Additionally, broad-based inflation, metals, energy and other commodity costs, transportation and logistics costs, labor costs, and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations all continue to impact our results.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing disruptions and shortages significantly impact revenue and cash flows.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are being managed, but there is a risk of obsolescence or shortage due to geopolitical factors.

evidence.text: As of March 31, 2023, Wabtec had approximately $14 million of assets related to Russian operations, which were primarily cash and inventory. Management has determined, based on information currently available, that these assets are expected to be recoverable and therefore no impairment has been recorded. This will continue to be monitored and may result in a future impairment charge based on changes in the situation. Management determined that inventory related to operations in Ukraine were not expected to be recoverable and were written off resulting in an insignificant charge during the first quarter of 2022.
evidence.subsection: Item 2.    MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

sentiment.label: neutral

sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include macroeconomic and supply chain challenges.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on Integration 2.0 and value creation through technology and market expansion.

evidence.text: During the first quarter of 2023, Wabtec continued to execute on our value creation framework by signing a strategic order for new locomotives in Brazil, securing a key order for mining drive systems in high altitude applications and delivering our 500th locomotive to Indian Railways - a significant milestone in our 10-year contract. Our senior unsecured debt was upgraded by Moody's, which reflects resiliency of the business, our balance sheet strength and strong cash generation. Additionally, Wabtec rebranded our Digital Electronics product line to Digital Intelligence; a change that more accurately reflects the complete digital products and services portfolio offered to our customers.

During three months ended March 31, 2023, the Company incurred one-time restructuring charges for programs included in the initiative of approximately $9 million, primarily for employee-related costs and asset write downs associated with site consolidations in Europe. factors.summary: Interest rate increases are impacting the company through higher borrowing costs.

evidence.text: Interest expense, net, increased $5 million for the three months ended March 31, 2023 compared to the same period in 2022 primarily due to higher effective interest rates in the current period.

sentiment.label: negative

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are affecting sales, particularly in international markets.

evidence.text: The following table shows the major components of the change in net sales in the three months ended March 31, 2023 from the three months ended March 31, 2022: In millions Freight Segment Transit Segment Total First Quarter 2022 Net sales $ 1,322 $ 605 $ 1,927 Acquisitions 21 — 21 Foreign Exchange (21) (35) (56) Organic 244 58 302 First Quarter 2023 Net sales $ 1,566 $ 628 $ 2,194

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements have led to a decrease in net sales, showing a negative impact on revenue.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks, such as the Russian invasion of Ukraine, are creating disruptions and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: The Russian invasion of Ukraine and the resultant sanctions related to Russia and Belarus have further impacted our supply and distribution channels and caused significant price inflation which had, and are expected to continue to have, adverse effects on Wabtec’s business results. As of March 31, 2023, Wabtec had approximately $14 million of assets related to Russian operations, which were primarily cash and inventory. Management has determined, based on information currently available, that these assets are expected to be recoverable and therefore no impairment has been recorded. This will continue to be monitored and may result in a future impairment charge based on changes in the situation. Management determined that inventory related to operations in Ukraine were not expected to be recoverable and were written off resulting in an insignificant charge during the first quarter of 2022.

evidence.text: The scope of the review includes consolidating our operating footprint, reducing headcount, streamlining the end-to-end manufacturing process, restructuring the North America distribution channels, expanding operations in low-cost countries and simplifying the business through systems enablement, including the source-to-pay process. Management will also consider additional capital investments to further simplify and streamline the business.
